items.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit record highs, Dow slips amid tech earnings and shifting oil prices.
items.summary: U.S. equities closed out the week with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite reaching new record highs on Friday, May 1, largely propelled by strong corporate earnings from major tech companies like Apple. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, experienced a slight decline. Market sentiment remains largely confident, fueled by the ongoing AI boom and robust earnings growth, although geopolitical tensions and the path of AI spending continue to be closely watched by investors. Oil prices saw a drop following a new peace proposal from Iran, easing some market concerns.

sections.title: Major Indices (as of market close May 1, 2026)
Top Movers (as of market close May 1, 2026)

items.headline: Apple surges on strong earnings and outlook.
items.summary: Apple Inc. saw its stock jump following a robust Q1 2026 earnings report, exceeding expectations with a 17% year-over-year revenue increase and a 22% climb in EPS. The company also announced a $100 billion stock buyback program and a 4% dividend hike.

items.headline: SanDisk revenues more than triple on AI demand.
items.summary: SanDisk Corp. experienced a significant rise after reporting quarterly revenue that more than tripled to $5.95 billion, with profits far exceeding expectations, driven by strong demand for NAND memory in AI systems. The company also announced a $6 billion share buyback.

items.headline: Atlassian boosts guidance after strong Q3.
items.summary: Atlassian Corp. shares soared after the software company reported a fiscal third-quarter beat and raised its full-year guidance for cloud and data center revenue.

items.headline: Amgen declines despite beating earnings estimates.
items.summary: Amgen Inc. saw its stock fall. Despite beating earnings estimates, the company's strong results were overshadowed by modest guidance targets, creating downward pressure on the Dow-weighted stock.

items.headline: Roblox plunges on reduced annual bookings forecast.
items.summary: Roblox Corp. shares plunged after the company cut its annual bookings forecast, disappointing investors.

items.headline: U.S. Economy Grows 2% in Q1 2026 Amid Geopolitical Headwinds.
items.summary: The U.S. economy expanded at a 2% annualized pace in the first quarter of 2026, marking an acceleration from the previous quarter but falling slightly short of economists' forecasts. Geopolitical tensions, particularly the ongoing conflict in Iran, and sustained high oil prices are noted as potential headwinds for future growth, keeping the Federal Reserve cautious on interest rate cuts.

items.headline: Spirit Airlines ceases operations after financial struggles.
items.summary: Low-cost carrier Spirit Airlines announced it would cease operations effective immediately on May 2, 2026. The airline had been battling escalating financial difficulties, including a failed bid for a $500 million federal bailout and increased competition, compounded by soaring jet fuel prices due to the Iran war.

items.headline: Big Tech ramps up AI investments, raising capex projections.
items.summary: Major tech companies like Meta, Alphabet, Amazon, and Microsoft are significantly increasing their capital expenditure projections for 2026 to fund massive AI infrastructure. Meta projects up to $145 billion, Alphabet $180-$190 billion, and Microsoft around $190 billion, while Amazon maintains its $200 billion plan. This extensive spending highlights the industry's commitment to AI but also raises investor scrutiny on future profitability.

items.headline: U.S. Treasury targets Iranian shadow banking networks.
items.summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designated three Iranian foreign currency exchange houses and their associated front companies as part of 'Economic Fury.' This action aims to disrupt financial lifelines supporting the Iranian regime's war efforts.

items.headline: AON reports strong Q1 2026 results and reaffirms guidance.
items.summary: AON plc delivered strong first-quarter 2026 results, with 5% organic revenue growth and total revenue increasing to $5.0 billion. Adjusted operating margin expanded by 70 basis points, and adjusted EPS rose 14% to $6.48. The company reaffirmed its 2026 guidance and plans over $1 billion in share repurchases.

items.headline: Berkshire Hathaway's Q1 2026 earnings due today.
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway is expected to release its quarterly report today, May 2, 2026. This report is anticipated to provide key insights into corporate earnings quality across various sectors, including insurance, rail transportation, and energy.

items.headline: Dow reports decreased net sales and loss per share in Q1.
items.summary: Dow Inc. reported Q1 2026 net sales of $9.8 billion, a 6% decrease year-over-year. Volume declined by 2%, impacted by the Middle East conflict. The company reported a GAAP net loss of $445 million and an operating loss per share of $0.14.
